claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let me help you check your eligibility for Child Benefit. 

To get started, I need to know about your child (or children):

**How old is the child you're claiming for?**

This is important because:
- Children **under 16** are generally automatically eligible
- Children **aged 16-19** may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Children **aged 20 or over** don't qualify

Please tell me the age of your child. If you have multiple children, we can check each one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit for my son, Alex. He's 8 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Thank you for that information. Let me check the eligibility for your son Alex, who is 8 years old.

Since Alex is under 16, he falls into the automatic qualification age category. However, I need to check one important condition:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

Since Alex is only 8 years old, I assume he's not in paid work at all. Can you confirm this? This is just a standard check - children under 16 who are working 24+ hours per week wouldn't qualify.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working at all. He's just 8, so he's at primary school!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! That's what I expected. Let me continue checking the eligibility requirements.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own right?**

Specifically, is he receiving Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit in his own name? 

Again, for an 8-year-old at primary school, I expect the answer is no, but I need to confirm this as it's part of the eligibility criteria.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't get any benefits himself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship?**

Since Alex is only 8 years old and in primary school, I'm confident he's not in an apprenticeship, but I need to confirm this for the assessment. Can you confirm he's not in any apprenticeship program?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, of course not! He's just 8 and at primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! Thank you for confirming. Now let's move on to check your eligibility as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to check that you meet the UK residency requirements. 

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

If you do, that's the simplest path to meet the residency requirement. 

Alternatively, you might qualify if you:
- Have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- Have pre-settled status with certain conditions (working, studying, or sufficient resources)
- Are living abroad as a Crown servant

Do you normally live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK at the moment. I'm living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since you're living abroad, let me check if you meet any of the special circumstances that would still allow you to claim Child Benefit.

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant?**

Crown servants include people working for:
- HM Forces
- UK civil service
- Other UK government departments or agencies

Or alternatively, do any of these apply to you:

1. **Do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

2. **Do you have pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?** (with certain conditions like working, studying, or having sufficient resources)

Please let me know which, if any, of these situations applies to you.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply to me. I'm not a Crown servant, and I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Based on the information you've provided, unfortunately you do not meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment Result

I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible** for Child Benefit at this time.

### For Alex (age 8):

While Alex meets all the child-related eligibility requirements:
- ✓ He's under 16 years old
- ✓ He's not in paid work
- ✓ He's not receiving benefits in his own right
- ✓ He's not in an apprenticeship

**However**, the claim cannot proceed because **you do not meet the UK residency requirements**.

### Why You're Not Eligible:

To claim Child Benefit, you must either:
- Normally live in the UK, **OR**
- Meet special circumstances such as:
  - Having settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
  - Having pre-settled status with qualifying criteria (working, studying, or having sufficient resources)
  - Being a Crown servant living abroad
  - Living in certain qualifying countries

Since you don't currently live in the UK and don't fall into any of the special circumstances, you cannot claim Child Benefit.

### What You Can Do:

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you move back to the UK or obtain settled/pre-settled status), you may become eligible at that time. You would need to reapply then.

If you have any questions about your specific situation or think there may be other circumstances that apply, you can contact HMRC's Child Benefit Office for further advice.

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship, which means he meets all the child-related qualifying criteria. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant does not normally live in the UK, is not a Crown servant living abroad, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Therefore, despite Alex being a qualifying child, the claim cannot proceed due to the claimant's residency status.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is seeking Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. While Alex meets all the child eligibility criteria (appropriate age, not working, not receiving benefits, not in apprenticeship), the claim is ineligible because the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. To claim Child Benefit, claimants must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances such as being a Crown servant abroad or having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, none of which apply in this case.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
